content.text: Hi - I'm trying to get `GEN=ninja make` build command to work per the DuckDB docs. However it appears to not interface correctly with jq's build process; see output of that command in
. Can you help me figure out the error?

content.thinking: Looking at the error, it seems the jq external project is being configured to use ninja as its build system, but the build directory doesn't actually have a build.ninja file generated yet. I need to check how the extension's CMakeLists.txt is setting up the jq external project to see if there's a configuration step missing or if the generator isn't being passed through correctly.

file.content:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.5)

set(TARGET_NAME jq)
set(EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_extension)
set(LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_loadable_extension)

project(${TARGET_NAME})

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD "17" CACHE STRING "C++ standard to enforce")
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

include_directories(src/include)

# --- libjq (built from the ./jq submodule via its own autotools build) ---
include(ExternalProject)

set(JQ_SRC_DIR     ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/jq)
set(JQ_BUILD_DIR   ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/jq-build)
set(JQ_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/jq-install)
set(JQ_STATIC_LIB    ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/lib/libjq.a)
set(ONIG_STATIC_LIB  ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/lib/libonig.a)

ExternalProject_Add(jq_external
  SOURCE_DIR        ${JQ_SRC_DIR}
  BINARY_DIR        ${JQ_BUILD_DIR}
  INSTALL_DIR       ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}
  CONFIGURE_COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E chdir ${JQ_SRC_DIR} autoreconf -i
            COMMAND ${JQ_SRC_DIR}/configure
                      --prefix=${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}
                      --disable-shared --enable-static --with-pic
                      --disable-maintainer-mode --disable-docs
                      --with-oniguruma=builtin
  BUILD_COMMAND      ${CMAKE_MAKE_PROGRAM}
  INSTALL_COMMAND    ${CMAKE_MAKE_PROGRAM} install
  BUILD_BYPRODUCTS   ${JQ_STATIC_LIB} ${ONIG_STATIC_LIB}
)

# Make headers visible at configure time (the dir exists after install; create
# it now so include_directories doesn't warn on first run).
file(MAKE_DIRECTORY ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/include)

add_library(libjq STATIC IMPORTED GLOBAL)
add_dependencies(libjq jq_external)
set_target_properties(libjq PROPERTIES
  IMPORTED_LOCATION             ${JQ_STATIC_LIB}
  INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/include
)

add_library(libonig STATIC IMPORTED GLOBAL)
add_dependencies(libonig jq_external)
set_target_properties(libonig PROPERTIES
  IMPORTED_LOCATION ${ONIG_STATIC_LIB}
)

set(EXTENSION_SOURCES src/jq_extension.cpp)

build_static_extension(${TARGET_NAME} ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})
build_loadable_extension(${TARGET_NAME} " " ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})

# Link libjq (with its bundled oniguruma) in both the static library and the loadable extension
target_link_libraries(${EXTENSION_NAME}          libjq libonig)
target_link_libraries(${LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME} libjq libonig)

install(
  TARGETS ${EXTENSION_NAME}
  EXPORT "${DUCKDB_EXPORT_SET}"
  LIBRARY DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}"
  ARCHIVE DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}")

content.thinking: I see the problem now—when using Ninja as the generator, `CMAKE_MAKE_PROGRAM` gets set to `ninja`, but jq's build system is based on autotools with its own Makefile, so it actually needs to be built with `make` instead. I need to explicitly override this to use the correct build tool.

content.text:
Found it. The `BUILD_COMMAND` / `INSTALL_COMMAND` use `${CMAKE_MAKE_PROGRAM}`, which becomes `ninja` when you set `GEN=ninja`. But jq is an autotools project — its `configure` script generates a `Makefile`, not a `build.ninja`, so ninja chokes with "loading 'build.ninja': No such file or directory".

Fix: hardcode `make` for the jq sub-build, since the generator-driven program doesn't apply to a foreign (autotools) build system.

content.content: The file /Users/tim_vergenz/code/personal/duckdb-jq/CMakeLists.txt has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.5)

set(TARGET_NAME jq)
set(EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_extension)
set(LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_loadable_extension)

project(${TARGET_NAME})

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD "17" CACHE STRING "C++ standard to enforce")
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

include_directories(src/include)

# --- libjq (built from the ./jq submodule via its own autotools build) ---
include(ExternalProject)

set(JQ_SRC_DIR     ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/jq)
set(JQ_BUILD_DIR   ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/jq-build)
set(JQ_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/jq-install)
set(JQ_STATIC_LIB    ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/lib/libjq.a)
set(ONIG_STATIC_LIB  ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/lib/libonig.a)

ExternalProject_Add(jq_external
  SOURCE_DIR        ${JQ_SRC_DIR}
  BINARY_DIR        ${JQ_BUILD_DIR}
  INSTALL_DIR       ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}
  CONFIGURE_COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E chdir ${JQ_SRC_DIR} autoreconf -i
            COMMAND ${JQ_SRC_DIR}/configure
                      --prefix=${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}
                      --disable-shared --enable-static --with-pic
                      --disable-maintainer-mode --disable-docs
                      --with-oniguruma=builtin
  BUILD_COMMAND      ${CMAKE_MAKE_PROGRAM}
  INSTALL_COMMAND    ${CMAKE_MAKE_PROGRAM} install
  BUILD_BYPRODUCTS   ${JQ_STATIC_LIB} ${ONIG_STATIC_LIB}
)

# Make headers visible at configure time (the dir exists after install; create
# it now so include_directories doesn't warn on first run).
file(MAKE_DIRECTORY ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/include)

add_library(libjq STATIC IMPORTED GLOBAL)
add_dependencies(libjq jq_external)
set_target_properties(libjq PROPERTIES
  IMPORTED_LOCATION             ${JQ_STATIC_LIB}
  INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/include
)

add_library(libonig STATIC IMPORTED GLOBAL)
add_dependencies(libonig jq_external)
set_target_properties(libonig PROPERTIES
  IMPORTED_LOCATION ${ONIG_STATIC_LIB}
)

set(EXTENSION_SOURCES src/jq_extension.cpp)

build_static_extension(${TARGET_NAME} ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})
build_loadable_extension(${TARGET_NAME} " " ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})

# Link libjq (with its bundled oniguruma) in both the static library and the loadable extension
target_link_libraries(${EXTENSION_NAME}          libjq libonig)
target_link_libraries(${LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME} libjq libonig)

install(
  TARGETS ${EXTENSION_NAME}
  EXPORT "${DUCKDB_EXPORT_SET}"
  LIBRARY DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}"
  ARCHIVE DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}")
